title: Parahebe muelleri
intro: Parahebe muelleri, also known as Mueller's Parahebe, is a perennial plant species native to New Zealand and Australia, belonging to the family Plantaginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and support overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Parahebe muelleri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Parahebe muelleri is commonly prepared as a tincture or tea, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
